prestazioni: Engine swap to a Rover K-Series KV6 (2.5L, 24-valve, naturally aspirated) with forged internals including Mahle Motorsport forged pistons (88.9mm bore, 10.5:1 CR), Eagle H-beam connecting rods, and a steel billet crankshaft from Quaife. Pair with a custom intake manifold by Jenvey Dynamics (individual throttle bodies, 45mm diameter) and a Haltech Elite 2500 ECU for precise fuel and ignition mapping.

Forced induction via a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger (0.63 A/R turbine housing) with a custom top-mount intercooler by Bell Auto Services (500x220x76mm core, aluminum end tanks) and a Tial MV-R 50mm external wastegate. Supporting mods include a custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust system by Scorpion Exhausts with a 200-cell catalytic converter and a MagnaFlow 76mm rear muffler for optimized flow.

Suspension overhaul with Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (24-way adjustable damping, 30mm drop) paired with Eibach Pro-Spacer wheel spacers (20mm front, 25mm rear) to correct scrub radius. Braking system upgrade to AP Racing CP6640 front calipers (4-piston, 330mm discs) and CP5350 rear calipers (2-piston, 280mm discs) with Ferodo DS2500 brake pads and Goodridge G-Stop braided stainless steel brake lines.
estetica: Full carbon fiber widebody conversion using a custom Rocket Bunny-inspired kit by KW Motorsport, featuring extended front fenders (30mm wider), rear quarter panels (50mm wider), and a custom front splitter with integrated canards. Rear diffuser by Seibon Carbon with dual 3-inch exhaust cutouts.

Custom 17x9 ET20 front and 17x10 ET25 rear wheels by Work Emotion CR Kiwami (gunmetal finish, 5x114.3 PCD) wrapped in Toyo R888R tires (225/45/17 front, 255/40/17 rear). Fender flares lined with custom LED turn signals by Diode Dynamics.

Interior stripped and rebuilt with a full Sparco Pro2000 racing seat (carbon fiber shell, FIA-approved) mounted on custom aluminum brackets, paired with a MOMO Mod.78 steering wheel (350mm diameter, suede grip) and a custom roll cage by Safety Devices (6-point, T45 steel, bolt-in design). Dashboard replaced with a digital display by AIM MXS Strada (1.3-inch screen, CAN bus integration).
